author: @Kangsteri
text: The guy passed away. He never did any extraordinary claims. He stated that they were experimental concept art, and he never even saw how long they could run. It was based the Oxford electric bell dry pile. Look for the Extreme Batteries video. It was moisture collecting non acidic set, with 10 different materials on it. Two of them are obviously graphite and silicone. His website can be found with Way back machine. The formula was patented, but I never found the patent on Google patents.

author: @selfcorrected-Bobby
text: i think homemade tetrateanite is a big solution for energy generation. instead of worrying about radioactive decay of energy sources on satellite probes flying through the solar system one wold worry about the magnetic decay of the tetrateanite's domains. Cost is certainly a key issue, and convenience and simplicity.

replies.author: @selfcorrected-Bobby
replies.text: and my personal opinion is we may not need batteries, just a variable clutch (to vary strength of curren, not only switch "off - actually it deproximates the inductabiity of elecricity in the coil from the still-spinning leitating rotor (in my ide). to move the coil away from the levitating rotor to switch "off" the charge from the generator, that is decouple the proximity of the coil from the spinning NSNS magnets which induce the AC charge in the coil.... the energy I think will last as long as those tetrateanite batteries remain floating and spinning... here we're talking about magnetic decay based on air resistance NS Gravity putting a stain on the levitating magnetic rotor... the optimal design really is what is of concern. The size of the base / footpiece stator and the size of the levitating rotor. So my idea, my doddy drive I always and can only obsess about, it wouln't be a battery, you just decouple the coil from the still spinning rotor. So actually you don't need a battery. Just a generator that does not stop spinning that can be decoupled from accepting an induced AC charge in a variable clutch coil, to set the strength of energy one would want, ... this talk was NOT what I was expecting LOL :) since it is just levitating tetrateanite magnets spinning in the air if it the generator is not "Switched" off it won't be making a sound as i is jus wirring through the room's air... One could make big ones for each home easily. Just it should be scaled to deliver X amount of Watts, amps, voltage, or whatever is needed, 240 Volts, amps. So obviously the basic design would need som circuitry, which I have zero knowledge of but is basic knowledge for electric egineers... still waiting on how to make tetrateanite magnes, as I'd like to mold my own magnets for my flying magnetic rotor... I would not want the rotor's field o interrupt the SNSN circular arrangement on the top or outer rim of the leviating rotor that is inducting current in the coil, but I'm not sure on how to do that, but the concept was explained in a video not too long ago, but I don't totally undestand...

author: @user-hf3ym7lh4d
text: Just for context, their website doesn't seem to exist any more and apparently the guy behind it died in 2021.

replies.author: @johnwynne-qx6br
replies.text: Yes someone in the comments of one of his videos said that he had died. His videos were/are great to watch. But as Robert says very expensive to buy one of his products although he did offer free small working models if you bought i think a brochure.

replies.author: @ryanjamesloyd6733
replies.text:
Damn, that's unfortunate.  His mostly permanent magnet motor was a really cool design (provided I'm thinking of the right guy)
He used an electromagnet to get it past the "gate" on each revolution and the permanent magnets and flywheel effect did all the rest of the turning. 
That concept seems like it would be useful.
